title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents a five-pillar framework for deploying enterprise AI agents: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. He illustrates with a retail bank case where evaluation data, tracing infrastructure, and a measurement pipeline were critical to transitioning a failed chatbot PoC to production, and where observability later caught policy drift causing stale answers.
writeup: Sandipan Bhaumik of Databricks lays out a five-pillar framework for deploying enterprise AI agents: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The talk grounds the framework in a retail bank case, where evaluation data, tracing infrastructure, and a measurement pipeline moved a failed chatbot proof of concept into production. It also shows observability catching policy drift that was producing stale answers after deployment. The takeaway: production readiness depends on measurement and monitoring infrastructure, not just the agent itself.
